rulings.Case Title: STEVE DUTRA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony Of Plaintiff Steve Dutra, Or, In The Alternative, To Re-Open The Deposition Of Plaintiff Steve Dutra
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Rexnord Industries¿ motion to re-open the deposition of Mr. Dutra is granted. The deposition must not taken longer than two hours (assuming that there is no delay due to Mr. Dutra or his counsel) and will take place at a location chosen by Mr. Dutra near his residence or in the Bay Area and is limited to Stearns brakes only. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THERESA HINKLE et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Imo Industries Inc.S Motion For An Order Compelling Plaintiffs To Serve Executed Stipulations For Nprc Civilian Records Of Decedent, And For Social Security Records Of Decedents Father
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MAY 25, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 206 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 1. (TRANSFERRED FROM DEPT. 220.) DEFENDANT WARREN PUMPS, LLC Joinder In Defendant Imo Industries Inc.S Motion For An Order Compelling Plaintiffs To Serve Executed Stipulations For Nprc Civilian Records Of Decedent, And For Social Security Records Of Decedents Father. Defendant IMO Industries Inc.'s motion to compel plaintiff to serve executed stipulations for NPRC Civilian Records of Decedent, and for social security records of decedent¿s father is denied. As to the NPRC records, the court cannot order a party to enter into a stipulation. As to the decedent¿s father¿s social security records, the form provided to plaintiff by Berry and Berry appears to require the signature of the person whose records are sought. =(206/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: THERESA HINKLE et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Union Carbide Corporation¿S Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatory No. 3 And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,290.00
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MAY 25, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 206 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 1. (TRANSFERRED FROM DEPT. 220.) DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatory No. 3 And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,290.00. Defendant Union Carbide¿s motion to compel further response to Special Interrogatory No.3 and request for monetary sanctions is granted. Plaintiffs are ordered to respond further within 15 days notice of this order. Plaintiffs must provide the current residential address and telephone numbers for each witness identified in its response to Special Interrogatory No. 3. If after a ¿diligent search and reasonable inquiry,¿ plaintiffs are unable to provide responsive answers, they shall ¿set forth the efforts made to secure the information¿ and state that they have no responsive information. CCP 2030.220; Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal.App.3d 771, 782. Plaintiffs¿ attorney of record, the law office of Keller, Fishback & Jackson LLP shall pay monetary sanctions of in the amount of $1,100.00 to council for Union Carbide: McKenna, Long & Aldridge LLP within 15 days. Sanctions are warranted because Plaintiffs¿ position lacked substantial justification. =(206/JJM)
